data.title: Write failing integration tests: Figma URL parsing and PAT authentication
data.description:
## What
Create tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh with failing integration tests for Figma URL parsing and PAT authentication. These tests MUST FAIL (RED) until figma-url-parse.sh and figma-auth.sh are implemented.

## testing_mode: RED
New test file. No prior coverage. Tests must be written before any implementation.

## Test Scenarios (Given/When/Then)

### URL Parsing (FP-URL-1 through FP-URL-4)
- FP-URL-1: Given a /design/-format URL, when figma-url-parse.sh processes it, then file key is extracted to stdout and exits 0
- FP-URL-2: Given a /file/-format URL, when figma-url-parse.sh processes it, then file key extracted, exits 0
- FP-URL-3: Given a /proto/-format URL, when figma-url-parse.sh processes it, then file key extracted, exits 0
- FP-URL-4: Given an invalid URL (no Figma domain, no key), when figma-url-parse.sh processes it, then exits 1 with error message on stderr

### PAT Authentication (FP-AUTH-1 through FP-AUTH-3)
- FP-AUTH-1: Given a valid PAT, when figma-auth.sh validates via GET /v1/me, then exits 0
- FP-AUTH-2: Given an invalid PAT, when figma-auth.sh validates, then exits 1 with re-provisioning instructions on stderr
- FP-AUTH-3: Given FIGMA_PAT env var and no config key, when figma-auth.sh runs, then reads PAT from env var (env-var fallback)

## File Structure
- Source tests/lib/assert.sh (existing)
- Read PAT from FIGMA_PAT env var (fallback for auth tests — stub with test value, expect failure)
- Script-not-found failures are acceptable as the RED state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh
- [ ] File contains test cases FP-URL-1 through FP-URL-4 (grep for labels in comments)
  Verify: grep -q 'FP-URL-1' tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh && grep -q 'FP-URL-4' tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh
- [ ] File contains test cases FP-AUTH-1 through FP-AUTH-3
  Verify: grep -q 'FP-AUTH-1' tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh && grep -q 'FP-AUTH-3' tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh
- [ ] Running the test file exits non-zero (tests FAIL — RED state confirmed)
  Verify: bash tests/integration/test-figma-pullback-integration.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0 (run-all treats this integration test file per its runner logic — non-zero integration tests reported but run-all still exits 0 if no hook/unit failures)
